place: Bedminster CDP, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Bedminster’s name traces back to Bedminster in Somerset, England, a district of Bristol.
The Pluckemin Continental Artillery Cantonment Site in Bedminster hosted America’s first artillery training academy during 1778‑79, a precursor to West Point.
General Henry Knox’s historic Vanderveer‑Knox House, used by him during the war, now serves as a museum and was listed on the National Register in 1995.
The North Branch of the Raritan River cuts through Bedminster, historically powering mills and shaping settlement patterns.
Bedminster’s township history spans from Dutch, German, and Scots‑Irish settlers in 1710 to its formal incorporation in 1798.
The Somerset Hills Learning Institute, founded in 1998, applies behavior analysis to educate children on the autism spectrum.
